NKJV.1: Brethren, if a man is overtaken in any trespass, you who are spiritual restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness, considering yourself lest you also be tempted.
NKJV.2: Bear one another’s burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.
NKJV.3: For if anyone thinks himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceives himself.
NKJV.4: But let each one examine his own work, and then he will have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another.
NKJV.5: For each one shall bear his own load.
NKJV.6: Let him who is taught the word share in all good things with him who teaches.
NKJV.7: Do not be deceived, God is not mocked; for whatever a man sows, that he will also reap.
NKJV.8: For he who sows to his flesh will of the flesh reap corruption, but he who sows to the Spirit will of the Spirit reap everlasting life.
NKJV.9: And let us not grow weary while doing good, for in due season we shall reap if we do not lose heart.
NKJV.10: Therefore, as we have opportunity, let us do good to all, especially to those who are of the household of faith.
NKJV.11: See with what large letters I have written to you with my own hand!
NKJV.12: As many as desire to make a good showing in the flesh, these would compel you to be circumcised, only that they may not suffer persecution for the cross of Christ.
NKJV.13: For not even those who are circumcised keep the law, but they desire to have you circumcised that they may boast in your flesh.
NKJV.14: But God forbid that I should boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
NKJV.15: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision avails anything, but a new creation.
NKJV.16: And as many as walk according to this rule, peace and mercy be upon them, and upon the Israel of God.
NKJV.17: From now on let no one trouble me, for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus.
NKJV.18: Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

ESV.1: Brothers,  if anyone is caught in any transgression, you who are spiritual should restore him in a spirit of gentleness. Keep watch on yourself, lest you too be tempted.
ESV.2: Bear one another's burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.
ESV.3: For if anyone thinks he is something, when he is nothing, he deceives himself.
ESV.4: But let each one test his own work, and then his reason to boast will be in himself alone and not in his neighbor.
ESV.5: For each will have to bear his own load.
ESV.6: Let the one who is taught the word share all good things with the one who teaches.
ESV.7: Do not be deceived: God is not mocked, for whatever one sows, that will he also reap.
ESV.8: For the one who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption, but the one who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.
ESV.9: And let us not grow weary of doing good, for in due season we will reap, if we do not give up.
ESV.10: So then, as we have opportunity, let us do good to everyone, and especially to those who are of the household of faith.
ESV.11: See with what large letters I am writing to you with my own hand.
ESV.12: It is those who want to make a good showing in the flesh who would force you to be circumcised, and only in order that they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.
ESV.13: For even those who are circumcised do not themselves keep the law, but they desire to have you circumcised that they may boast in your flesh.
ESV.14: But far be it from me to boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by which  the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
ESV.15: For neither circumcision counts for anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.
ESV.16: And as for all who walk by this rule, peace and mercy be upon them, and upon the Israel of God.
ESV.17: From now on let no one cause me trouble, for I bear on my body the marks of Jesus.
ESV.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.

NASB.1: Brethren, even if anyone is caught in any trespass, you who are spiritual, restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness; [each one] looking to yourself, so that you too will not be tempted.
NASB.2: Bear one another's burdens, and thereby fulfill the law of Christ.
NASB.3: For if anyone thinks he is something when he is nothing, he deceives himself.
NASB.4: But each one must examine his own work, and then he will have [reason for] boasting in regard to himself alone, and not in regard to another.
NASB.5: For each one will bear his own load.
NASB.6: The one who is taught the word is to share all good things with the one who teaches [him].
NASB.7: Do not be deceived, God is not mocked; for whatever a man sows, this he will also reap.
NASB.8: For the one who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption, but the one who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.
NASB.9: Let us not lose heart in doing good, for in due time we will reap if we do not grow weary.
NASB.10: So then, while we have opportunity, let us do good to all people, and especially to those who are of the household of the faith.
NASB.11: See with what large letters I am writing to you with my own hand.
NASB.12: Those who desire to make a good showing in the flesh try to compel you to be circumcised, simply so that they will not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.
NASB.13: For those who are circumcised do not even keep the Law themselves, but they desire to have you circumcised so that they may boast in your flesh.
NASB.14: But may it never be that I would boast, except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
NASB.15: For neither is circumcision anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.
NASB.16: And those who will walk by this rule, peace and mercy [be] upon them, and upon the Israel of God.
NASB.17: From now on let no one cause trouble for me, for I bear on my body the brand-marks of Jesus.
NASB.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brethren. Amen.

NLT.1: Dear brothers and sisters, if another believer is overcome by some sin, you who are godly should gently and humbly help that person back onto the right path. And be careful not to fall into the same temptation yourself.
NLT.2: Share each other’s burdens, and in this way obey the law of Christ.
NLT.3: If you think you are too important to help someone, you are only fooling yourself. You are not that important.
NLT.4: Pay careful attention to your own work, for then you will get the satisfaction of a job well done, and you won’t need to compare yourself to anyone else.
NLT.5: For we are each responsible for our own conduct.
NLT.6: Those who are taught the word of God should provide for their teachers, sharing all good things with them.
NLT.7: Don’t be misled — you cannot mock the justice of God. You will always harvest what you plant.
NLT.8: Those who live only to satisfy their own sinful nature will harvest decay and death from that sinful nature. But those who live to please the Spirit will harvest everlasting life from the Spirit.
NLT.9: So let’s not get tired of doing what is good. At just the right time we will reap a harvest of blessing if we don’t give up.
NLT.10: Therefore, whenever we have the opportunity, we should do good to everyone — especially to those in the family of faith.
NLT.11: NOTICE WHAT LARGE LETTERS I USE AS I WRITE THESE CLOSING WORDS IN MY OWN HANDWRITING.
NLT.12: Those who are trying to force you to be circumcised want to look good to others. They don’t want to be persecuted for teaching that the cross of Christ alone can save.
NLT.13: And even those who advocate circumcision don’t keep the whole law themselves. They only want you to be circumcised so they can boast about it and claim you as their disciples.
NLT.14: As for me, may I never boast about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Because of that cross, my interest in this world has been crucified, and the world’s interest in me has also died.
NLT.15: It doesn’t matter whether we have been circumcised or not. What counts is whether we have been transformed into a new creation.
NLT.16: May God’s peace and mercy be upon all who live by this principle; they are the new people of God.
NLT.17: From now on, don’t let anyone trouble me with these things. For I bear on my body the scars that show I belong to Jesus.
NLT.18: Dear brothers and sisters, may the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

WEB.1: Brothers, even if a man is caught in some fault, you who are spiritual must restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness; looking to yourself so that you also aren’t tempted.
WEB.2: Bear one another’s burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.
WEB.3: For if a man thinks himself to be something when he is nothing, he deceives himself.
WEB.4: But let each man examine his own work, and then he will have reason to boast in himself, and not in someone else.
WEB.5: For each man will bear his own burden.
WEB.6: But let him who is taught in the word share all good things with him who teaches.
WEB.7: Don’t be deceived. God is not mocked, for whatever a man sows, that he will also reap.
WEB.8: For he who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption. But he who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.
WEB.9: Let us not be weary in doing good, for we will reap in due season, if we don’t give up.
WEB.10: So then, as we have opportunity, let’s do what is good toward all men, and especially toward those who are of the household of the faith.
WEB.11: See with what large letters I write to you with my own hand.
WEB.12: As many as desire to look good in the flesh, they compel you to be circumcised; only that they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.
WEB.13: For even they who receive circumcision don’t keep the law themselves, but they desire to have you circumcised, that they may boast in your flesh.
WEB.14: But far be it from me to boast, except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
WEB.15: For in Christ Jesus neither is circumcision anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.
WEB.16: As many as walk by this rule, peace and mercy be on them, and on God’s Israel.
WEB.17: From now on, let no one cause me any trouble, for I bear the marks of the Lord Jesus branded on my body.
WEB.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.

CSB17.1: Brothers and sisters, if someone is overtaken in any wrongdoing, you who are spiritual, restore such a person with a gentle <sup>ⓐ</sup> spirit, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup> watching out for yourselves so that you also won’t be tempted. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.2: Carry one another’s burdens; in this way you will fulfill the law of Christ. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.3: For if anyone considers himself to be something when he is nothing, <sup>ⓔ</sup> he deceives himself.
CSB17.4: Let each person examine his own work, <sup>ⓕ</sup> and then he can take pride in himself alone, and not compare himself with someone else.
CSB17.5: For each person will have to carry his own load.
CSB17.6: Let the one who is taught the word share <sup>ⓖ</sup> all his good things with the teacher.
CSB17.7: Don’t be deceived: God is not mocked. For whatever a person sows he will also reap,
CSB17.8: because the one who sows to his flesh will reap destruction from the flesh, but the one who sows to the Spirit <sup>ⓗ</sup> will reap eternal life <sup>ⓘ</sup> from the Spirit.
CSB17.9: Let us not get tired <sup>ⓙ</sup> of doing good, <sup>ⓚ</sup> for we will reap at the proper time if we don’t give up.
CSB17.10: Therefore, as we have opportunity, let us work <sup>ⓛ</sup> for the good <sup>ⓜ</sup> of all, especially for those who belong to the household <sup>ⓝ</sup> of faith. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.11: Look at what large letters I use as I write to you in my own handwriting.
CSB17.12: Those who want to make a good impression in the flesh <sup>ⓑ</sup> are the ones who would compel you to be circumcised <sup>ⓒ</sup>  — but only to avoid being persecuted <sup>ⓓ</sup> for the cross <sup>ⓔ</sup> of Christ.
CSB17.13: For even the circumcised don’t keep <sup>ⓕ</sup> the law themselves, and yet they want you to be circumcised in order to boast about your flesh.
CSB17.14: But as for me, I will never boast about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. The world has been crucified to me through the cross, and I to the world.
CSB17.15: For <sup>[2]</sup> both circumcision and uncircumcision <sup>ⓖ</sup> mean nothing; what matters instead is a new creation. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.16: May peace come to all those who follow this standard, and mercy even to the Israel <sup>ⓘ</sup> of God! <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.17: From now on, let no one cause me trouble, because I bear on my body the marks of Jesus. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.18: Brothers and sisters, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. <sup>ⓚ</sup> Amen.

AMP.1: Brothers, if anyone is caught in any sin, you who are spiritual [that is, you who are responsive to the guidance of the Spirit] are to restore such a person in a spirit of gentleness [not with a sense of superiority or self-righteousness], keeping a watchful eye on yourself, so that you are not tempted as well.
AMP.2: Carry one another's burdens and in this way you will fulfill the requirements of the law of Christ [that is, the law of Christian love].
AMP.3: For if anyone thinks he is something [special] when [in fact] he is nothing [special except in his own eyes], he deceives himself.
AMP.4: But each one must carefully scrutinize his own work [examining his actions, attitudes, and behavior], and then he can have the personal satisfaction and inner joy of doing something commendable without comparing himself to another.
AMP.5: For every person will have to bear [with patience] his own burden [of faults and shortcomings for which he alone is responsible].
AMP.6: The one who is taught the word [of God] is to share all good things with his teacher [contributing to his spiritual and material support].
AMP.7: Do not be deceived, God is not mocked [He will not allow Himself to be ridiculed, nor treated with contempt nor allow His precepts to be scornfully set aside]; for whatever a man sows, this and this only is what he will reap.
AMP.8: For the one who sows to his flesh [his sinful capacity, his worldliness, his disgraceful impulses] will reap from the flesh ruin and destruction, but the one who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.
AMP.9: Let us not grow weary or become discouraged in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap, if we do not give in.
AMP.10: So then, while we [as individual believers] have the opportunity, let us do good to all people [not only being helpful, but also doing that which promotes their spiritual well-being], and especially [be a blessing] to those of the household of faith (born-again believers).
AMP.11: See with what large letters I am writing to you with my own hand.
AMP.12: Those who want to make a good impression in public [before the Jews] try to compel you to be circumcised, just so they will escape being persecuted for [faithfulness to] the cross of Christ.
AMP.13: For even the circumcised [Jews] themselves do not [really] keep the Law, but they want to have you circumcised so that they may boast in your flesh [that is, in the fact that they convinced you to be circumcised].
AMP.14: But far be it from me to boast [in anything or anyone], except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
AMP.15: For neither is circumcision anything [of any importance], nor uncircumcision, but [only] a new creation [which is the result of a new birth—a spiritual transformation—a new nature in Christ Jesus].
AMP.16: Peace and mercy be upon all who walk by this rule [who discipline themselves and conduct their lives by this principle], and upon the [true] Israel of God (Jewish believers).
AMP.17: From now on let no one trouble me [by making it necessary for me to justify my authority as an apostle, and the absolute truth of the gospel], for I bear on my body the branding-marks of Jesus [the wounds, scars, and other outward evidence of persecutions—these testify to His ownership of me].
AMP.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, my brothers and sisters. Amen.

MSG.1: Live creatively, friends. If someone falls into sin, forgivingly restore him, saving your critical comments for yourself. You might be needing forgiveness before the day's out.
MSG.2: Stoop down and reach out to those who are oppressed. Share their burdens, and so complete Christ's law.
MSG.3: If you think you are too good for that, you are badly deceived.
MSG.4: Make a careful exploration of who you are and the work you have been given, and then sink yourself into that. Don't be impressed with yourself. Don't compare yourself with others.
MSG.5: Each of you must take responsibility for doing the creative best you can with your own life.
MSG.6: Be very sure now, you who have been trained to a self-sufficient maturity, that you enter into a generous common life with those who have trained you, sharing all the good things that you have and experience.
MSG.7: Don't be misled: No one makes a fool of God. What a person plants, he will harvest. The person who plants selfishness, ignoring the needs of others—ignoring God!—
MSG.8: harvests a crop of weeds. All he'll have to show for his life is weeds! But the one who plants in response to God, letting God's Spirit do the growth work in him, harvests a crop of real life, eternal life.
MSG.9: So let's not allow ourselves to get fatigued doing good. At the right time we will harvest a good crop if we don't give up, or quit.
MSG.10: Right now, therefore, every time we get the chance, let us work for the benefit of all, starting with the people closest to us in the community of faith.
MSG.11: Now, in these last sentences, I want to emphasize in the bold scrawls of my personal handwriting the immense importance of what I have written to you.
MSG.12: These people who are attempting to force the ways of circumcision on you have only one motive: They want an easy way to look good before others, lacking the courage to live by a faith that shares Christ's suffering and death. All their talk about the law is gas.
MSG.13: They themselves don't keep the law! And they are highly selective in the laws they do observe. They only want you to be circumcised so they can boast of their success in recruiting you to their side. That is contemptible!
MSG.14: For my part, I am going to boast about nothing but the Cross of our Master, Jesus Christ. Because of that Cross, I have been crucified in relation to the world, set free from the stifling atmosphere of pleasing others and fitting into the little patterns that they dictate.
MSG.15: Can't you see the central issue in all this? It is not what you and I do—submit to circumcision, reject circumcision. It is what God is doing, and he is creating something totally new, a free life!
MSG.16: All who walk by this standard are the true Israel of God—his chosen people. Peace and mercy on them!
MSG.17: Quite frankly, I don't want to be bothered anymore by these disputes. I have far more important things to do—the serious living of this faith. I bear in my body scars from my service to Jesus.
MSG.18: May what our Master Jesus Christ gives freely be deeply and personally yours, my friends. Oh, yes!

NIV.1:
Doing Good to All
Brothers, if someone is caught in a sin, you who are spiritual should restore him gently. But watch yourself, or you also may be tempted.
NIV.2: Carry each other’s burdens, and in this way you will fulfill the law of Christ.
NIV.3: If anyone thinks he is something when he is nothing, he deceives himself.
NIV.4: Each one should test his own actions. Then he can take pride in himself, without comparing himself to somebody else,
NIV.5: for each one should carry his own load.
NIV.6: Anyone who receives instruction in the word must share all good things with his instructor.
NIV.7: Do not be deceived: God cannot be mocked. A man reaps what he sows.
NIV.8: The one who sows to please his sinful nature, from that nature will reap destruction; the one who sows to please the Spirit, from the Spirit will reap eternal life.
NIV.9: Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.
NIV.10: Therefore, as we have opportunity, let us do good to all people, especially to those who belong to the family of believers.
NIV.11:
Not Circumcision but a New Creation
See what large letters I use as I write to you with my own hand!
NIV.12: Those who want to make a good impression outwardly are trying to compel you to be circumcised. The only reason they do this is to avoid being persecuted for the cross of Christ.
NIV.13: Not even those who are circumcised obey the law, yet they want you to be circumcised that they may boast about your flesh.
NIV.14: May I never boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
NIV.15: Neither circumcision nor uncircumcision means anything; what counts is a new creation.
NIV.16: Peace and mercy to all who follow this rule, even to the Israel of God.
NIV.17: Finally, let no one cause me trouble, for I bear on my body the marks of Jesus.
NIV.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.

NRSVCE.1: My friends, if anyone is detected in a transgression, you who have received the Spirit should restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness. Take care that you yourselves are not tempted.
NRSVCE.2: Bear one another’s burdens, and in this way you will fulfill the law of Christ.
NRSVCE.3: For if those who are nothing think they are something, they deceive themselves.
NRSVCE.4: All must test their own work; then that work, rather than their neighbor’s work, will become a cause for pride.
NRSVCE.5: For all must carry their own loads.
NRSVCE.6: Those who are taught the word must share in all good things with their teacher.
NRSVCE.7: Do not be deceived; God is not mocked, for you reap whatever you sow.
NRSVCE.8: If you sow to your own flesh, you will reap corruption from the flesh; but if you sow to the Spirit, you will reap eternal life from the Spirit.
NRSVCE.9: So let us not grow weary in doing what is right, for we will reap at harvest time, if we do not give up.
NRSVCE.10: So then, whenever we have an opportunity, let us work for the good of all, and especially for those of the family of faith.
NRSVCE.11: See what large letters I make when I am writing in my own hand!
NRSVCE.12: It is those who want to make a good showing in the flesh that try to compel you to be circumcised — only that they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.
NRSVCE.13: Even the circumcised do not themselves obey the law, but they want you to be circumcised so that they may boast about your flesh.
NRSVCE.14: May I never boast of anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
NRSVCE.15: For neither circumcision nor uncircumcision is anything; but a new creation is everything!
NRSVCE.16: As for those who will follow this rule — peace be upon them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.
NRSVCE.17: From now on, let no one make trouble for me; for I carry the marks of Jesus branded on my body.
NRSVCE.18: May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers and sisters. Amen.

NET.1: Brothers and sisters, if a person is discovered in some sin, you who are spiritual restore such a person in a spirit of gentleness. Pay close attention to yourselves, so that you are not tempted too.
NET.2: Carry one another’s burdens, and in this way you will fulfill the law of Christ.
NET.3: For if anyone thinks he is something when he is nothing, he deceives himself.
NET.4: Let each one examine his own work. Then he can take pride in himself and not compare himself with someone else.
NET.5: For each one will carry his own load.
NET.6: Now the one who receives instruction in the word must share all good things with the one who teaches it.
NET.7: Do not be deceived. God will not be made a fool. For a person will reap what he sows,
NET.8: because the person who sows to his own flesh will reap corruption from the flesh, but the one who sows to the Spirit will reap eternal life from the Spirit.
NET.9: So we must not grow weary in doing good, for in due time we will reap, if we do not give up.
NET.10: So then, whenever we have an opportunity, let us do good to all people, and especially to those who belong to the family of faith.
NET.11: See what big letters I make as I write to you with my own hand!
NET.12: Those who want to make a good showing in external matters are trying to force you to be circumcised. They do so only to avoid being persecuted for the cross of Christ.
NET.13: For those who are circumcised do not obey the law themselves, but they want you to be circumcised so that they can boast about your flesh.
NET.14: But may I never boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
NET.15: For neither circumcision nor uncircumcision counts for anything; the only thing that matters is a new creation!
NET.16: And all who will behave in accordance with this rule, peace and mercy be on them, and on the Israel of God.
NET.17: From now on let no one cause me trouble, for I bear the marks of Jesus on my body.
NET.18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers and sisters. Amen.
